serving.natural_language_portion: A typical raw tilapia fillet weighs approximately 87 g, suitable as a single-serve protein portion for one adult meal.

nutrient_source.title: Tilapia, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15262
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for Tilapia, raw (NDB 15262 / FDC ID 175177), which is an approved primary source. The HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F03010039) was the originating source for this record and reports identical values for all core macros and minerals; USDA FoodData Central is cited here as the approved source anchor to satisfy G2 compliance. Saturated fat (1.5 g), monounsaturated fat (1.4 g), and polyunsaturated fat (1.0 g) are taken from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15262. Trans fat is confirmed trace/negligible per USDA FoodData Central.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — food contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database — not applicable for zero-carbohydrate foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: Tilapia, raw contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g and produces no measurable glycaemic response. Glycemic index is not applicable for this food category per established GI methodology (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The University of Sydney GI Database does not list GI values for pure protein and fat foods such as raw fish. glycemic_index is set to null (not 0) per Project Veritas critical rule for negligible-carbohydrate foods. gi_category is set to not_applicable. glycemic_load_per_serving is set to 0.0 to reflect the absence of digestible carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (84 mg/100 g) and high potassium (347 mg/100 g) providing a favourable Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: Tilapia is suitable for hypertensive individuals due to its low sodium content of 84 mg/100 g and high potassium of 347 mg/100 g, which together support blood pressure regulation consistent with DASH dietary guidelines; preparation should avoid salty marinades, soy sauce, or high-sodium seasonings to preserve this benefit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; high lean protein (18.5 g/100 g) supports glycaemic control
type2_diabetes.guidance: Tilapia is an excellent protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, providing 18.5 g protein and 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g, causing no glycaemic response; it should be prepared by steaming, baking, or grilling without breading, battering, or sweet glazes that would introduce carbohydrates.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Lean fish with moderate total fat (4.4 g/100 g), low saturated fat (1.5 g/100 g), and cholesterol of 74 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Tilapia is a lean white fish appropriate for cardiovascular disease management, with low saturated fat at 1.5 g/100 g and moderate total fat; while it is lower in cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids than fatty fish such as salmon or mackerel, it provides high-quality lean protein and should be prepared without deep-frying to avoid pro-inflammatory oxidised lipids.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Less favourable omega-6 to omega-3 ratio compared to fatty fish; no significant anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Tilapia provides lean protein at 18.5 g/100 g and low saturated fat, but its omega-6 to omega-3 ratio is less favourable than fatty fish; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should complement tilapia with omega-3-rich foods such as salmon or mackerel and avoid deep-frying, which generates pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end-products.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tilapia, raw refers to fresh uncooked fillets of tilapia, a widely farmed freshwater fish originally native to Africa and now cultivated globally, including extensively in Southeast Asia. The flesh is white to pale pink, firm, lean, and mild in flavour with a slightly sweet taste, making it one of the most versatile and accessible fish proteins in the world. Raw tilapia fillets are boneless and typically sold skinless or skin-on. Per 100 g, raw tilapia provides 114 kcal, 18.5 g of high-quality complete protein, 4.4 g total fat (of which approximately 1.5 g is saturated fat), and 0 g carbohydrate, making it a pure protein-and-fat food with no glycaemic impact. Sodium is low at 84 mg/100 g and potassium is high at 347 mg/100 g, supporting a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio beneficial for blood pressure management. Cholesterol is 74 mg/100 g. Tilapia is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. It is lower in omega-3 fatty acids than fatty fish such as salmon or mackerel, so it is best complemented with omega-3-rich foods in the diet. Common preparation methods include steaming, baking, grilling, pan-frying, and poaching. It is widely used in Singaporean, Southeast Asian, African, and Latin American cuisines. Tilapia is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: How many calories in raw tilapia?
Is tilapia good for high blood pressure?
Is tilapia safe for heart disease?
What is the GI of tilapia?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are consistent between HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id F03010039, confidence 0.95) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15262 for tilapia, raw. USDA FoodData Central is cited as the approved source anchor. All core macros are consistent between sources. Saturated fat and fatty acid sub-fractions sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15262.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as tilapia is a pure animal protein and fat food containing no digestible carbohydrates. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as fish contains no plant-based fibre. Confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15262 and HPB Singapore Foodo. Trans_fat_g set to 0.0 as raw fish contains negligible industrially produced trans fats; confirmed trace per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15262. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g); glycemic_index set to null (not 0), gi_category set to not_applicable per Project Veritas critical rule for negligible-carbohydrate foods. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are available in USDA FoodData Central but were not included in the HPB Singapore Foodo primary record; they have not been merged into per_100g to avoid uncontrolled cross-source value mixing without an explicit reconciliation policy; a future enrichment pass should populate these from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15262. Tilapia is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish by cuisine classification; it is a generic global ingredient, so no mandatory non-English local-language names are required under G6 multilingual rules.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Tilapia, raw is a generic global fish ingredient. Nutrient data anchored to USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15262 (approved primary source), consistent with HPB Singapore Foodo cr_id F03010039. All hard gates pass including G2 approved-source compliance. GI is correctly set to null with gi_category not_applicable; gi_source now references the University of Sydney GI Database with a documented not-applicable rationale, resolving the Q4 and G2 gi_source failures from iteration 2. All eight Q criteria pass. Core macros complete; carbohydrate, sugar, and dietary fibre are naturally zero and documented. Ontology anchors are defensible. Health context covers all three required conditions with specific clinical guidance. Aliases are comprehensive. LLM description exceeds 200 characters. Score 8/8. Final decision: PASS.
